case inr
A B : Islander
hAB : Islander.Said A (Islander.isKnave A ∨ Islander.isKnave B)
hA : Islander.isKnave A
this : ¬(Islander.isKnave A ∨ Islander.isKnave B)
⊢ Islander.isKnight A ∧ Islander.isKnave B

proof: <;> (try simp_all [isKnave_notisKnightIff, isKnight_notisKnaveIff]) <;> (try tauto) <;> (try exfalso; apply this; tauto)

    have h₃ : ¬B.isKnave := by

      have h₅ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := Or.inr h₄
      exact h₂ h₅
    have h₄ : A.isKnight := notisKnave_isKnight A hA
    exfalso
    have h₅ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := by
      have h₆ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := by
        simp [hA] at hAB ⊢
        <;> simp_all [isKnight_notisKnave]
        <;> tauto
      exact h₆
    exact h₂ h₅

simp only [not_or] at h₁
    exfalso
    have h₂ := h₁.1
    have h₃ := h₁.2
    -- Since A is a knave, A's statement must be false. Hence, ¬(A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave) is true.
    -- This means both A and B cannot be knaves, but A is already a knave, so B cannot be a knave.
    -- This leads to a contradiction because A being a knave implies A's statement is false, so B can't be a knave.
    -- But we can directly use the contradiction derived from h₁.
    apply h₁.1
    exact hA

theorem knave_said2 {A : Islander} {P : Prop} : A said P → A.isKnave → ¬ P := by 
  intro AP
  intro AKnave
  intro hP
  have AKnight := said_knight AP hP
  -- Use the fact that A cannot be both a knight and a knave to derive a contradiction
  have h : ¬ (A.isKnight ∧ A.isKnave) := not_isKnight_and_isKnave A
  have h₁ : A.isKnight := AKnight
  have h₂ : A.isKnave := AKnave
  exact h ⟨h₁, h₂⟩
```lean

```lean
example {A B : Islander} (hAB : A said (A.isKnave or B.isKnave)) : A.isKnight and B.isKnave := by
  knight_or_knave A with hA hA
  · -- Case: A is a knight
    obtain hA' | hB := knight_said hAB hA
    · -- Subcase: A is a knight and A is a knave, which is impossible
      exfalso
      have h1 : A.isKnight := hA
      have h2 : A.isKnave := hA'
      exact not_isKnight_and_isKnave A ⟨h1, h2⟩
    · -- Subcase: A is a knight and B is a knave
      exact ⟨hA, hB⟩
  · -- Case: A is a knave
    have h₁ : ¬(A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave) := by
      have h₂ : A said (A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave) := hAB
      have h₃ : A.isKnave := hA
      have h₄ : ¬(A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave) := by
        intro h
        have h₅ : ¬(A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave) := by
          intro h₆
          have h₇ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := h₆
          have h₈ : ¬(A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave) := by
            intro h₉
            exact knave_said hAB hA h₉
          exact h₈ h₇
        exact h₅ h
      exact h₄
    exfalso
    have h₂ : A.isKnave := hA
    have h₃ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := Or.inl h₂
    exact h₁ h₃

theorem knave_said2 {A : Islander} {P : Prop} : A said P → A.isKnave → ¬ P := by 
  intro AP
  intro AKnave
  intro hP
  have AKnight := said_knight AP hP
  -- Use the fact that A cannot be both a knight and a knave to derive a contradiction
  have h : ¬ (A.isKnight ∧ A.isKnave) := not_isKnight_and_isKnave A
  have h₁ : A.isKnight := AKnight
  have h₂ : A.isKnave := AKnave
  exact h ⟨h₁, h₂⟩
```lean

```lean
example {A B : Islander} (hAB : A said (A.isKnave or B.isKnave)) : A.isKnight and B.isKnave := by
  knight_or_knave A with hA hA
  · -- Case: A is a knight
    obtain hA' | hB := knight_said hAB hA
    · -- Subcase: A is a knight and A is a knave, which is impossible
      exfalso
      have h1 : A.isKnight := hA
      have h2 : A.isKnave := hA'
      exact not_isKnight_and_isKnave A ⟨h1, h2⟩
    · -- Subcase: A is a knight and B is a knave
      exact ⟨hA, hB⟩
  · -- Case: A is a knave
    have h₁ : ¬(A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave) := by
      have h₂ : A said (A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave) := hAB
      have h₃ : A.isKnave := hA
      have h₄ : ¬(A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave) := by
        intro h
        have h₅ : ¬(A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave) := by
          intro h₆
          have h₇ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := h₆
          have h₈ : ¬(A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave) := by
            intro h₉
            exact knave_said hAB hA h₉
          exact h₈ h₇
        exact h₅ h
      exact h₄
    exfalso
    have h₂ : A.isKnave := hA
    have h₃ : A.isKnave ∨ B.isKnave := Or.inl h₂
    exact h₁ h₃
